signals.raw:
King Charles Confirms Late April 2026 White House State Visit with Trump Despite Tensions | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Buckingham Palace announced on Tuesday that King Charles and Queen Camilla's first state visit to the US—his first—will occur in late April, with details forthcoming; Trump quickly confirmed April 27-30 dates including a White House banquet on April 28th[4][2].

signals.headline: **US Annual Core Inflation Surge**: Annual core rate hit 3
signals.raw: **US Annual Core Inflation Surge**: Annual core rate hit 3.9%, lifting 3-month annualized core PCE (Fed's preferred gauge) to 4.56%—over double the 2% target—prompting Fed discussions of rate hikes as Warsh's potential first move[1].

signals.headline: **Fed Policy Stance**: Wednesday meeting held rates at 3
signals.raw: **Fed Policy Stance**: Wednesday meeting held rates at 3.50-3.75%, projecting one 2026 cut, but dot plot shows 7/19 officials expect no cuts; markets now price ~0% cut odds, 20% hike chance by Dec 2026 per JPMorgan[1].

signals.headline: **Kevin Warsh Context**: Trump's nomination adds uncertainty; Warsh views "inflation as a choice" from excess spending/p
signals.raw: **Kevin Warsh Context**: Trump's nomination adds uncertainty; Warsh views "inflation as a choice" from excess spending/printing, favors liquidity reduction first[2][4].

signals.headline: **South Korea Link**: Bank of Korea holds at 2
signals.raw: **South Korea Link**: Bank of Korea holds at 2.2% CPI (Mar) via subsidies, but expects ~3% by May/June amid Iran war energy risks, watching Fed/Warsh for won pressure and capital flows[2].

signals.headline: **Prediction Market Impact**: Derivatives reflect hawkish repricing (cuts near zero, hikes possible); no formal confirma
signals.raw: **Prediction Market Impact**: Derivatives reflect hawkish repricing (cuts near zero, hikes possible); no formal confirmations/announcements today, but inflation data and nomination fuel volatility[1][3]. Global core CPI forecast stable at 2.8% (US at 3.2%)[5].
